response.justification: Sarah Smith is an experienced full-stack and web engineer whose career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — C++ appears only in a bachelor thesis project and a brief 6-month stint writing components at National Instruments over a decade ago. The role demands deep, current C++ expertise combined with industrial automation domain knowledge, secure development lifecycle practices (IEC 62443), and systems-level skills (multi-threading, MQTT, industrial protocols) that are entirely absent from her profile. Her event-driven and distributed-systems work is real but web-oriented, and does not compensate for the fundamental mismatch in primary language and domain.
strengths: Genuine event-driven and distributed-systems experience through GISCollective and Dunnhumby high-throughput micro-services work
Strong REST API design and web-services background that partially overlaps with the IoT/REST requirement
Demonstrated agile discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — and a track record of mentoring and knowledge sharing
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from her current skill set; it surfaces only in legacy, peripheral roles and is not listed among her core skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443 security standards, or Secure Development Lifecycle experience whatsoever
No exposure to required industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or low-level multi-threaded systems programming in C++
